name: Cinnamon Swirl Bread
method: MAKE SPONGE:
Heat milk to around 100 degrees in the microwave or on the stovetop.
Warm milk, flour, and yeast are mixed in a small bowl until thoroughly
blended. Allow it to sit for 45 to 60 minutes, or until air pockets have
developed in the sponge.
MAKE DOUGH:
Add eggs, flour, sugar, water, and salt to the bowl of a stand
mixer fitted with a hook attachment after the sponge has
developed some air pockets. Mix on medium speed until well
mixed, then gradually raise to medium-high speed and continue
mixing, scraping down the bowl every 4 to 5 minutes, for 10 to
13 minutes, or until dough pulls away from the edges of the
bowl and becomes glossy and elastic.
While the mixer is running, gently add the butter, 1 tablespoon
at a time, allowing each tablespoon to integrate thoroughly
before adding the next. Continue mixing medium-high speed for
5 to 7 minutes, or until the dough passes the windowpane test.
(This will take 13 to 15 minutes.) Once the dough has reached
this point, stir in the raisins until they are evenly distributed
throughout the dough.
Cover bowl tightly with plastic wrap and set aside for 1 hour or
until it has doubled in size.
ASSEMBLE AND BAKE LOAF:
Sugar and cinnamon should be whisked together in a small
bowl. Remove from the equation.
Roll out the dough onto a well-floured work surface into a big
rectangle, about 8" x 16". Fold the short ends inside as if you
were folding a letter. Turn the dough 90 degrees and roll out
another 8" × 16" sheet from the short end.
Pull up on the covered end and roll the dough into a tight spiral.
Insert the role seam side down in a buttered 8 "x5" loaf pan,
cover, and let rise for 1 to 2 hours, or until dough begins to rise
over the edge of the pan.
preheat the oven to 375°F and spray the top of the dough with
egg wash. Bake for 30 minutes or until deeply brown on top and
center registers between 190° and 205°F. Let rest for 5 minutes
before turning the loaf out onto a cooling rack to cool fully.
